Operation 'get_metadata_core_label' has semantic quality issues: vague_summary, vague_description, missing_context, missing_output_details. Original summary and description are overly generic and lack domain context. Enhanced version specifies that these are system-defined labels used for resource categorization, clarifies the return format, and explains the practical use case for AI agents. Added context about which resources use labels and when this operation should be called.

data.summary_suggestion: Fetch available system labels for resource categorization
data.description_suggestion: Retrieves the complete list of system-defined labels available for categorizing Dalil resources such as leads, contacts, accounts, and deals. Returns an array of label objects containing label identifiers, names, and descriptions. No authentication parameters required beyond standard bearer token. Use this operation to populate label selection dropdowns or validate label assignments in resource management workflows.
data.reasoning: Original summary and description are overly generic and lack domain context. Enhanced version specifies that these are system-defined labels used for resource categorization, clarifies the return format, and explains the practical use case for AI agents. Added context about which resources use labels and when this operation should be called.

message: Operation 'get_metadata_core_stage' has semantic quality issues: vague_summary, vague_description, missing_context, missing_output_details. Original summary and description lack specificity about the domain context. Enhanced version clarifies that stages are used for deal pipelines and workflows, specifies the return structure, provides examples of typical stage values, and explains when an AI agent should call this operation. This helps agents understand the relationship between stages and deals.

data.summary_suggestion: Fetch available deal and workflow stages
data.description_suggestion: Retrieves the complete list of system-defined stages used in deal pipelines and workflow processes within Dalil. Returns an array of stage objects containing stage identifiers, names, sequence order, and metadata. Stages typically represent progression states in deal lifecycles (e.g., prospecting, negotiation, closed). Use this operation to understand valid stage values for deal creation, filtering, or status updates.
data.reasoning: Original summary and description lack specificity about the domain context. Enhanced version clarifies that stages are used for deal pipelines and workflows, specifies the return structure, provides examples of typical stage values, and explains when an AI agent should call this operation. This helps agents understand the relationship between stages and deals.

data.summary_suggestion: Fetch available priority levels for resource management
data.description_suggestion: Retrieves the complete list of system-defined priority levels used for categorizing and prioritizing Dalil resources such as leads, contacts, deals, and tasks. Returns an array of priority objects containing priority identifiers, names, and ranking values. Typical priorities include low, medium, high, and critical. Use this operation to validate priority assignments, populate priority selection controls, or filter resources by priority level.
data.reasoning: Original summary and description are too minimal and lack domain context. data.summary_suggestion: Synchronize calendar events from external source
data.description_suggestion: Initiates the first-time synchronization of calendar events from an external calendar source into Dalil. This operation fetches all calendar events from the connected calendar provider and imports them into the system. Requires authentication and an active calendar integration. Returns the count of synchronized events and any errors encountered during the sync process. This operation may take several minutes depending on the number of events.
data.reasoning: Original description is too vague and lacks critical details. data.current_description: Clean up integration app customer data
data.summary_suggestion: Remove integration app customer and associated data
data.description_suggestion: Removes a customer's integration app account and all associated data from the Dalil system. This operation performs a complete cleanup including deletion of the customer record, related resources, and integration tokens. This is a destructive operation that cannot be undone. Requires authentication and appropriate permissions. Returns confirmation of successful deletion. Use with caution as this permanently removes all customer data.
data.reasoning: Original description lacks clarity about scope and consequences. Improved version specifies what gets deleted, emphasizes the destructive nature, clarifies authentication/permission requirements, explains the return value, and includes a warning for AI agents to understand the severity of this operation.
